summary: Encrypted payload decrypted and executed at require-time, targeting AI-agent sandboxes
details: Package advertises itself as TypeScript form-validation utilities, but the main entry (index.js) contains a top-level try-block that runs whenever the module is require()'d. It fingerprints the host (hostname equals 'devbox' or 'ubuntu-fc-uvm', presence of /app/.git, or env var JULES_SESSION_ID — the marker for Google's Jules AI coding-agent sandbox), reads lib/.perf.dat, AES-256-CBC-decrypts it with a hardcoded key/IV, and executes the plaintext via `new Function(_r)()`. Module identifiers are split at runtime (`require('f'+'s')`, `require('crypt'+'o')`, `require('pat'+'h')`, `createDecipheriv('aes-256-cb'+'c',...)`) specifically to defeat keyword-based scanners. The payload is hidden in a dot-prefixed file (lib/.perf.dat) under a 'performance telemetry' cover comment. The form-validation cover story has no relationship to crypto/fs/Function() machinery; the gating to AI-agent and CI microVM hostnames keeps the dropper dormant on developer laptops and detonates only inside automated coding-agent sandboxes where elevated tooling and network access are typically available. The decrypted bytes are attacker-controlled code executed in the consumer's process.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
